title: kubic repo: "[MIRROR] libslirp-4.3.1-1.el8.x86_64.rpm: Downloading successful, but checksum doesn't match."
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug



**Description**

The __kubic__ repo contains some internal checksum mismatch for the package __libslirp__.

I discovered that when I put the installation instructions for Centos 8:
https://podman.io/getting-started/installation
```
# CentOS 8
sudo dnf -y module disable container-tools
sudo dnf -y install 'dnf-command(copr)'
sudo dnf -y copr enable rhcontainerbot/container-selinux
sudo curl -L -o /etc/yum.repos.d/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable.repo https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/CentOS_8/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable.repo
sudo dnf -y install podman
```
in a Dockerfile (but with `sudo` removed)
<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1.

```
$ cat /etc/centos-release
CentOS Linux release 8.2.2004 (Core) 
$ ls
Dockerfile
$ cat Dockerfile
FROM docker.io/library/centos:8
RUN dnf -y update && \
    dnf -y module disable container-tools && \
    dnf -y install 'dnf-command(copr)' && \
    dnf -y copr enable rhcontainerbot/container-selinux && \
    curl -L -o /etc/yum.repos.d/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable.repo https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/CentOS_8/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable.repo && \
    dnf -y install podman
$ podman build -t centospodman .
```





**Describe the results you received:**

The command `podman build -t centospodman .` failed. The relevant error seems to be

```
[MIRROR] slirp4netns-1.1.4-1.el8.x86_64.rpm: Downloading successful, but checksum doesn't match. Calculated: 44974ede47ec7db75852725c3c62b4a6d720025af39d0b426c7ca1d78698b7c8(sha256)  Expected: 824505ad6cb0fec0b5a38b488d3e331098fd4ffbd1d955629d1e827b8ed98c76(sha256) 
(21/23): podman-2.0.5-1.el8.x86_64.rpm                                                                   21 MB/s |  23 MB     00:01    
[MIRROR] slirp4netns-1.1.4-1.el8.x86_64.rpm: Downloading successful, but checksum doesn't match. Calculated: 44974ede47ec7db75852725c3c62b4a6d720025af39d0b426c7ca1d78698b7c8(sha256)  Expected: 824505ad6cb0fec0b5a38b488d3e331098fd4ffbd1d955629d1e827b8ed98c76(sha256) 
[MIRROR] slirp4netns-1.1.4-1.el8.x86_64.rpm: Downloading successful, but checksum doesn't match. Calculated: 44974ede47ec7db75852725c3c62b4a6d720025af39d0b426c7ca1d78698b7c8(sha256)  Expected: 824505ad6cb0fec0b5a38b488d3e331098fd4ffbd1d955629d1e827b8ed98c76(sha256) 
[MIRROR] slirp4netns-1.1.4-1.el8.x86_64.rpm: Downloading successful, but checksum doesn't match. Calculated: 44974ede47ec7db75852725c3c62b4a6d720025af39d0b426c7ca1d78698b7c8(sha256)  Expected: 824505ad6cb0fec0b5a38b488d3e331098fd4ffbd1d955629d1e827b8ed98c76(sha256) 
[FAILED] slirp4netns-1.1.4-1.el8.x86_64.rpm: No more mirrors to try - All mirrors were already tried without success  
```



**Describe the results you expected:**

I would have expeced the command `podman build -t centospodman .` to succeed.


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.0.5
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.13.4
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


comments.body:
Looks like multiple builds of the same package, from different hosts (and with different checksums)

https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/CentOS_8/repodata/

OBS is stuck atm, but i'll rebuild a new one asap.
should be fixed now. Closing this, please reopen if unfixed on your side.
slirp4netns-1.1.4-2 and libslirp-4.3.1-2 are the builds with the fix

Still facing problems when installing podman via kubic repo
``` bash
sudo dnf -y module disable container-tools
sudo dnf -y install 'dnf-command(copr)'
sudo dnf -y copr enable rhcontainerbot/container-selinux
sudo curl -L -o /etc/yum.repos.d/devel:kubic:libcontainers:testing.repo https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/testing/CentOS_8/devel:kubic:libcontainers:testing.repo
sudo dnf -y install podman
```

@lz006 could you try a `dnf clean all` and then retry installing podman. I can install slirp4netns correctly on a clean centos8 env.
hi, you were right. It must be caused by some kind of tmp files. It is working. Thanks for quick response.

Hello, having a similar problem here on CentOS 8. Should I open a new issue? It seems there is some still undiscovered underlying motive for those breakages, since they keep coming back.

```
# dnf clean all
31 files removed
# dnf install -y podman
```

Update: The Centos_8_stream repo works fine. Centos_8 (not stream) gives above error.

@mirandadam I'm able to install podman on a fresh centos_8 (Not stream) just fine using the install steps. 

If a `dnf clean all` alone doesn't help, I'd suggest this:
```
# dnf clean all
# rm -rf /var/cache/dnf
# rpmdb --rebuilddb
# dnf -y install podman
```
 
If that doesn't help either, I guess this is probably an OBS issue and would need to be reported there.

Indeed, I can no longer reproduce the problem today. I did try "dnf clean all" yesterday before my first post, and I also tried with a fresh vm, I should have mentioned that. Also CentOS_8_stream doesn't work today anymore and gives "Error: Unable to find a match: podman".

Thanks for the super fast reply and the troubleshooting tips, @lsm5 ! If it happens again I will open an issue as you suggested. Just for clarification, what is OBS and where is it's repository so I could report issues?
